comment_text.sn1.32:7.1: Similar imagery is used at Dhp 21, where it is the “heedful” who do not die.
comment_text.sn1.32:8.4: Compare the parable of the widow’s two coins (Mark 12:41–44).

root_text.sn1.32:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.sn1.32:1.2: Atha kho sambahulā satullapakāyikā devatāyo abhikkantāya rattiyā abhikkantavaṇṇā kevalakappaṁ jetavanaṁ obhāsetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu.
root_text.sn1.32:1.3: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho ekā devatā bhagavato santike imaṁ gāthaṁ abhāsi:
root_text.sn1.32:3.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavato santike imā gāthāyo abhāsi:
root_text.sn1.32:6.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavato santike imā gāthāyo abhāsi:
root_text.sn1.32:9.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavato santike imā gāthāyo abhāsi:
root_text.sn1.32:12.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavato santike etadavoca:
root_text.sn1.32:13.1: “Sabbāsaṁ vo subhāsitaṁ pariyāyena; api ca mamapi suṇātha—
root_text.sn1.32:15.1: Atha kho aparā devatā bhagavantaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:

translation_text.sn1.32:0.2: 4. The Host of a Hundred Felicities
translation_text.sn1.32:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.sn1.32:1.2: Then, late at night, several glorious deities of the host of a hundred felicities, lighting up the entire Jeta’s Grove, went up to the Buddha, bowed, and stood to one side.
translation_text.sn1.32:1.3: Standing to one side, one deity spoke this verse in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.32:3.1: Then another deity recited these verses in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.32:4.1: “When a miser fails to give because of fear,
translation_text.sn1.32:4.2: the very thing they’re afraid of comes to pass.
translation_text.sn1.32:4.6: in this world and the next.
translation_text.sn1.32:5.2: overcoming that stain, and give a gift.
translation_text.sn1.32:5.3: The good deeds of sentient beings
translation_text.sn1.32:5.4: support them in the next world.”
translation_text.sn1.32:6.1: Then another deity recited these verses in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.32:7.1: “Among the dead they do not die,
translation_text.sn1.32:7.2: those who, like fellow travelers on the road,
translation_text.sn1.32:7.3: are happy to provide, though they have but little.
translation_text.sn1.32:8.1: Some who have little are happy to provide,
translation_text.sn1.32:8.2: while some who have much don’t wish to give.
translation_text.sn1.32:9.1: Then another deity recited these verses in the Buddha’s presence:
translation_text.sn1.32:10.3: the wicked don’t act like this,
translation_text.sn1.32:10.4: for the teaching of the good is hard to follow.
translation_text.sn1.32:11.1: That’s why the virtuous and the wicked
translation_text.sn1.32:11.4: while the virtuous are bound for heaven.”
translation_text.sn1.32:12.1: Then another deity said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn1.32:13.1: “You’ve all spoken well in your own way. However, listen to me also:
translation_text.sn1.32:14.1: A hundred thousand people <j>making a thousand sacrifices
translation_text.sn1.32:14.2: isn’t worth a fraction of that sort,
translation_text.sn1.32:14.3: who lives rightly, wandering for gleanings,
translation_text.sn1.32:14.4: or one who supports their partner <j>with what little they have.”
translation_text.sn1.32:15.1: Then another deity addressed the Buddha in verse:
translation_text.sn1.32:16.1: “Why doesn’t that sacrifice of theirs, so abundant and magnificent,
translation_text.sn1.32:16.2: equal the value of a moral person’s gift?
translation_text.sn1.32:16.3: How is it that a hundred thousand people  <j>making a thousand sacrifices
translation_text.sn1.32:16.4: isn’t worth a fraction of what’s offered by that sort?”
translation_text.sn1.32:17.4: in no way equals the value of a moral person’s gift.
translation_text.sn1.32:18.1: That’s how it is that a hundred thousand people  <j>making a thousand sacrifices
translation_text.sn1.32:18.2: isn’t worth a fraction of what’s offered by that sort.”

variant_text.sn1.32:11.1: asataṁ → asatañca (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed, pts2ed)
variant_text.sn1.32:16.3: Kathaṁ → idaṁ padaṁ si, pts1ed, pts2ed potthakesu
